reason: Satirical spin on nuclear dread served with manic, hilarious precision.
Kafkaesque absurdity of bureaucracy that’s as funny as it is terrifying.
Irreverent send-up of fate and faith with anarchic, gleeful wit.
Teenage bile and sharp satire collide in a deliciously dark comedy.
Politics and PR in the crosshairs of sharp, gallows-humored irony.
Small-town crime meets deadpan humor in a deliciously twisted tale.
War’s absurdity laid bare with a wisecracking, irreverent edge.
Violent satire that laughs at apocalypse in neon bullets.
Midnight laughs and full-moon horror blend in wickedly sharp balance.
Morbid slapstick that finds comedy in darkly absurd circumstances.
